verdict_reasoning: BAX is canonically a cytosolic/mitochondrial-outer-membrane pro-apoptotic protein. However, during apoptosis and immunogenic cell death, BAX translocates from the cytosol to the mitochondrial outer membrane and, critically, has documented exposure on the outer leaflet of the plasma membrane of dying/apoptotic cells. This cell-death-induced PM translocation qualifies as contextual surface accessibility under the cell_state_induced bucket. Multiple studies have detected BAX on the extracellular face of apoptotic tumor cells, enabling antibody-based targeting approaches.
